title: Versioning, Delete Markers, Object Lock, and Recovery · Go Semantics Case: Versioning State Transition
summary: A team enables versioning after an incident, assumes delete markers are backups, and applies compliance retention without testing irreversible governance and restore procedures. Predict the representation, storage, trust, cost, and recovery evidence before inspecting a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the versioning state transition increment in version and immutable-retention recovery runbook. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging…
objectives: Distinguish unversioned, enabled, and suspended versioning and test null versions, overwrite behavior, and lifecycle impact.
Chunk batch operations, correlate per-object outcomes, retry only eligible items, preserve authorization, and report partial success accessibly.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Predict Versioning State Transition · Theory Versioning State Transition
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go file-delivery team handles case 6726 during a regional dependency incident. Build deterministic Go evidence to validate version identity, immutable retention meaning, and executed restore evidence; record cleanup, rollback, restoration, and residual-risk ownership; then cancel halfway through and prove every body, part, and goroutine reaches a terminal state. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Versioning State Transition contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and Go Predict Versioning State Transition · Theory Versioning State Transition.
